mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing market, as indicated by a surge in Houst (HOUST) proxy, signals increased demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in housing market booms are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate, as indicated by a drop in UNRATE proxy, signals increased labor market tightness and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in labor market tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the industrial production, as indicated by an increase in INDPRO proxy, signals increased supply of goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side expansions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the gross domestic product, as indicated by a drop in GDPC1 proxy, signals reduced aggregate demand and decreased consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in demand-side contractions are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the monetary policy tightening, as indicated by an increase in FEDFUNDS proxy, signals increased interest rates and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in monetary policy tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the labor market participation rate, as indicated by a drop in PAYEMS proxy, signals reduced labor force and decreased consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in labor market participation declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the consumer price index, as indicated by an increase in CPIAUCSL proxy, signals increased inflation and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in inflationary environments are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the supply of money, as indicated by an increase in RSAFS proxy, signals increased aggregate demand and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side expansions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
